title_quote: Spearman ρ=0.315 concordance with Leaden Δfur signal confirms constitutive derepression (n=93 DEGs)
issue: The title attaches two overclaims to a single modest correlation. First, 'confirms' is strong-register for ρ=0.315 — the speaker_notes themselves concede the speaker 'must be ready to defend ρ=0.315 against that is a modest correlation.' REPORT §Finding 1 frames this result more conservatively as 'confirming Fur derepression as a major driver' (a major component, not the sole driver). Second, and worse, the title says 'confirms constitutive derepression' — but constitutivity (Δfur allele vs iron-limitation response) is throughline sub-claim A2, explicitly graded 'partial / single growth condition,' and it is established by the iron-discrimination comparison on the NEXT slide (slide 8), NOT by this concordance scatter. The figure shows correlation with Leaden's Δfur signal; it does not by itself discriminate constitutive derepression from an iron-status readout. The title imports a partial, single-condition conclusion into a confident verb over a 0.315 correlation.

report_evidence.quote: correlates with Leaden 2018's Δfur signal at Spearman ρ = 0.315, p = 2.08e-03 over 93 Leaden Δfur DEGs, with 71% sign concordance — confirming Fur derepression as a major driver
signal is constitutive Δfur derepression, not an iron-limitation response ... ⚠ partial — single growth condition (PYE rich)

fix_hint: Rewrite to scope the verb to what the scatter shows and drop 'constitutive' from this slide: 'Spearman ρ=0.315 (p=2.08e-03, 71% sign concordance) confirms Fur derepression as a major component of our signal (n=93 Leaden DEGs).' Reserve the constitutive-vs-iron claim for slide 8 where the iron-discrimination evidence actually appears.
